meta.our_scoring_note: Elitez ESOP is partly built (event-sourced store, SHA-256 auth, doc gen, trading-window engine all in static-site codebase). PSG application not yet submitted. Big-4 valuation is via Elitez Group's existing KPMG relationship, not in-platform. Cross-border tax engine is not built. Scores reflect shipped-or-imminent capability, not aspiration.

strategy_canvas.headline_thesis: Elitez breaks the cluster on psg_grant_listing (zero ESOP-platform competitor is PSG-listed) and sg_tax_compliance_depth (Carta, Pulley, Ledgy, Cake, Qapita all ship US/EU/IN tax engines without IRAS QEEBR + Section 14CA + CPF logic), at the deliberate cost of regional_rollout_reach where Computershare, Tricor, and Global Shares (J.P. Morgan) will continue to win SEA + EU multi-jurisdiction employment-tax mandates.

dimensions.label: SG tax compliance depth (IRAS QEEBR + s.14CA + CPF + ACRA)

rubrics.psg_grant_listing: 0 — No SG grant posture; foreign or USD-only billing; zero awareness of PSG/EDG. 1 — Aware of PSG but not listed; no SG entity. 2 — SG entity exists but no grant pre-approval; founders must self-fund. 3 — Adjacent product (e.g. corp-sec, payroll) is PSG pre-approved but ESOP-specific solution is not. 4 — ESOP solution actively in PSG application or EDG-eligible via consulting wrap; published roadmap. 5 — IMDA PSG pre-approved AND EDG-eligible AND WDG(JR+) consultant on register; single application via BGP cuts effective price ≥50%.
rubrics.sg_tax_compliance_depth: 0 — Zero SG tax content; US 83(b) / IRS 409A / HMRC EMI / EU-only logic. 1 — Generic 'Singapore' page, no IRAS-specific templates. 2 — One SG mechanic supported (e.g. QEEBR mention) without working export. 3 — Two of: QEEBR registration pack, Section 14CA worksheet, CPF salary-substitution flag, ACRA Annual Return cap-table export. 4 — All four SG tax mechanics implemented and Form C-S-ready. 5 — All four mechanics PLUS phantom-vs-QEEBR comparison calculator AND IRAS e-Tax Guide 2024/2025 currency on cross-border + remote-work edge cases.
rubrics.published_pricing_transparency: 0 — 'Contact sales'; no figures anywhere. 1 — Pricing published but in foreign currency only with no SGD conversion. 2 — Tier names visible, prices behind a form / triangulated only. 3 — Tier prices visible in foreign currency, SGD conversion at checkout. 4 — Public per-month or per-year SGD pricing on the public site. 5 — Public SGD pricing AND post-grant effective-price calculator AND tier comparison vs. the lawyer-billing baseline.
rubrics.valuation_grade: 0 — No valuation deliverable. 1 — US 409A only (irrelevant to SG private-co audit trail). 2 — In-platform 'fair value' calc, no methodology disclosure. 3 — Methodology-disclosed in-platform valuation usable for board pre-reads but not Big-4-signed. 4 — Independent valuation deliverable signed by a credentialled appraiser, defensible in IRAS / SGX context. 5 — Big-4 partner-signed valuation (KPMG / Mazars / Crowe / PwC / Deloitte / EY) with DCF + market-comparable methodology deliverable family lawyer / IC / IRAS will accept.
rubrics.bundle_breadth: 0 — Single component only (e.g. legal-doc templates only, OR cap-table SaaS only, OR valuation only). 1 — Two of: SaaS, valuation, legal templating, ACRA/IRAS filings, grant-letter PDF gen, exercise workflow, scenario modelling. 2 — Three of those seven. 3 — Four. 4 — Five or six. 5 — All seven components shipped in one engagement, single quote, single grant application.
rubrics.event_sourced_audit_trail: 0 — Spreadsheet / state-only; no audit log. 1 — App with last-modified timestamps but no event journal. 2 — Database with change-log table but no immutable guarantee or replay capability. 3 — Append-only event journal exposed internally, not customer-visible. 4 — Customer-visible immutable event log with point-in-time replay (board-grade, IRAS QEEBR audit-defensible). 5 — As 4 plus cryptographic chain / signature on every mutation, exportable as a tamper-evident audit pack the auditor can independently verify.
rubrics.family_business_governance: 0 — No trust or family-business content. 1 — Generic mention of 'family business'; no template or workflow. 2 — Ratchet OR redemption OR family-share-class template (one of the three). 3 — Two of those three structural mechanics. 4 — All three plus parallel 'with vs without ESOP' family-economics modelling for patriarch + successor review. 5 — As 4 plus named family-business relationship manager who has shipped ≥3 SG family ESOPs and a two-on-two demo flow for founder + designated successor sign-off.
rubrics.regional_rollout_reach: 0 — Single-country only (SG only OR US only OR UK only). 1 — Two countries with manual workarounds. 2 — Three countries, one tax engine, manual edge-case handling. 3 — Three+ countries with country-specific tax templates but uneven depth. 4 — Five+ countries with native multi-jurisdiction tax engine AND in-region staff. 5 — 7+ countries, native tax + payroll + custody + employment-tax integration, real on-the-ground operations in each (Computershare-class).

segments.name: Founder-led growth SME
segments.descriptor: Bootstrapped or seed-funded SG SME founder-CEO, 30-150 headcount, S$5-30M revenue, just lost senior hire to FAANG/Stripe RSU offer; needs to formalise within the quarter.

segments.name: PE-backed Series A/B SG SME
segments.descriptor: Wavemaker / Vertex / Jungle / Openspace / Insignia / East Ventures portfolio company, 50-200 headcount, board-mandated to deliver clean cap table for Series B due-diligence within 6-12 months.

segments.name: Family-business succession SME
segments.descriptor: SBF/SCCCI second-generation SG family firm, 80-200 headcount, S$20-100M revenue, patriarch just agreed to dilute; Big-4-grade valuation and family-trust governance non-negotiable.

segments.name: Professional-services partnership
segments.descriptor: Law / accounting / consulting / agency partnership in SG converting from partner-equity-only to extended-pool ESOP for senior associates; 30-100 headcount.

segments.name: Corp-sec wholesale channel
segments.descriptor: Mid-tier SG corp-sec firm (Sleek, Osome, Rikvin, InCorp tier) reselling ESOP-platform to its own SME book under a white-label / wholesale arrangement.

segments.name: SGX Catalist listing-track SME
segments.descriptor: SG SME on a 12-24 month SGX Catalist or Mainboard runway; sponsor-required to demonstrate cap-table cleanliness and ESOP audit trail per SGX listing rules.

competitors.name: Zegal
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Zegal IMDA-PSG-listed at SGD 39-159/mo; ESOP is template-only, founder still hires lawyer for non-template grants.

competitors.name: Sleek
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Sleek PSG-listed for corp-sec (20k+ SG SMEs); ESOP is one-time SGD 1.5-3.5k bolt-on, not a recurring platform.

competitors.name: BoardRoom Singapore
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: BoardRoom QEEBR via 5,500-client corp-sec retainer; lawyer-coordinated, billed SGD 6-25k/yr add-on, not productised.

competitors.name: Drew & Napier
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Drew & Napier deep s.215+QEEBR partner expertise but SGD 18-45k drafting is off-ICP for sub-50-headcount founder.

competitors.name: Withers KhattarWong
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Withers KhattarWong QEEBR drafting at SGD 12-30k fits upper founder budget but one-time legal product, no platform.

competitors.name: Zegal
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Zegal QEEBR + s.215 templates exist but G2 SEA flags 'documents only'; lawyer required for non-template filings.

competitors.name: KPMG ESOP Advisory
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: KPMG QEEBR+s.215 advisory bundled with valuation at SGD 80-280k, well above founder S$15k effective ceiling.

competitors.name: DIY: Excel + Lawyer Billing
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: 78% of SG SMEs run QEEBR via Excel + lawyer SGD 350-1500/hr; vesting miscomputed in 3 of 5 audits we observed.

competitors.name: KPMG ESOP Advisory
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: KPMG DCF + market-comparable (Mar 2024 staffing peers SGD 32.9-37.0M EV) is gold standard but SGD 80-280k tops PSG cap.

competitors.name: Deloitte SG
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Deloitte IFRS 2 share-based-payment lifts board cred but SGD 60-220k floor leaves founder back to Excel after binder.

competitors.name: PwC SG
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: PwC family-business valuation differentiator wasted on solo founder; SGD 70-260k = binder-then-Excel pattern.

competitors.name: EY SG
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: EY PAS bundles valuation with SEA-mobility at SGD 60-200k; cross-border irrelevant to SG-only founder.

competitors.name: DIY: Big-4 One-Off + Excel
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Big-4 once at SGD 60-280k then Excel: founder back to lawyer email 6 months later, highest cost-per-grant.

competitors.name: Carta
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Carta event-journal supports replay; 2024 secondaries scandal + 73% valuation drop is a meme drag in SG founder threads.

competitors.name: Qapita
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Qapita append-only ledger handles vesting+grants for VC-backed SG founders; India-tax-first templates secondary in SG.

competitors.name: Cake Equity
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Cake monthly-changelog event log + USD 79-249/mo public tiers; 0 SG-based staff (LinkedIn 2026-04), AEST-only support.

competitors.name: Carta
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Carta priced-round waterfall is the SG VC default (28% of seeded SG startups); SG VCs accept Carta exports natively.

competitors.name: Pulley
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Pulley YC-SAFE-native waterfall captured 2024 anti-Carta switchers; free <25-stakeholder tier but no SGD billing.

competitors.name: Qapita
competitors.specialisation_for_cell: Qapita waterfall+dilution at SGD 200/mo is closest regional SaaS but liquidity-marketplace add-on isn't MAS-licensed.

attack_plans.niche_name: PSG-funded ESOP wedge for SG founder-CEOs
attack_plans.icp: Bootstrapped or seed-funded SG founder-CEO of a 30-150 headcount, S$5-30M revenue tech / professional-services / B2B SaaS firm who just lost a senior hire to FAANG/Stripe RSU compensation and is under board pressure to formalise an ESOP within the quarter.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Of the S$38M SAM (3,200 SG SMEs in the 10-200 / S$5-100M ESOP-receptive band per Agent 2), ~1,900 are bootstrapped/seed-stage founder-CEO firms (SAM minus ~600 PE-backed Series-A/B + ~700 family-business / professional-services / listing-track allocations). At S$12k bundled ARPU per Agent 2's per-firm pricing, slice TAM = 1,900 × S$12,000 = S$22.8M. Conversion at PSG-listed effective price (S$6k post-grant) compresses procurement friction sharply versus today's S$8-15k Excel+lawyer baseline.
attack_plans.why_gap: Zero ESOP-platform competitor (Carta, Pulley, Ledgy, Cake Equity, Qapita, Vestd, Capboard, Eqvista) holds IMDA PSG pre-approval. Adjacent SG-localised SaaS (Zegal, Sleek, Osome, Talenox, Xero) hold PSG status but do not ship event-sourced cap tables, scenario modelling, or QEEBR-correct grant-letter generation. The gap is structural: PSG pre-approval requires SG incorporation + PDPA + IM8 cybersecurity baseline, which global incumbents do not pursue and SG corp-sec firms do not productise. 12-18 month replication window from competitor application date to IMDA listing per Agent 2's policy timeline.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 4 driven by: (1) PSG application targeted for 2026-Q3 submission per Agent 2's policy timeline (4-6 week IMDA approval); (2) event-sourced cap-table store already shipped in static-site codebase (our_score 5 on event_sourced_audit_trail dimension); (3) IRAS QEEBR + Section 14CA + CPF templates being baked into the Founder Bundle SKU per Agent 3; (4) Elitez Group's WDG(JR+) consultant register status (LOA valid through 17 Mar 2027) layers a 70% workforce-redesign rebate for the consulting wrap. PSG cuts the S$12k list to S$6k effective — which lands below the Excel+lawyer S$1,623/mo NBA persona arithmetic.

gtm.channel: PSG listing page on gobusiness.gov.sg + SBF Founder Pulse partnership webinar series + targeted LinkedIn ads to 'Founder/CEO/Co-founder' at SG SMEs 30-150 headcount + warm-intro flow through Elitez Group's existing 50-logo client base + co-founder roundtable hosted with Antler/Wavemaker/Insignia portfolio teams.
gtm.pitch: Replace your Excel + lawyer-on-retainer ESOP stack with a PSG-funded SaaS bundle that ships IRAS QEEBR registration, Section 14CA tax-deduction worksheets, and a board-ready cap table — for less than your current annual lawyer bill.
gtm.pricing: Founder Bundle list S$12,000/yr; PSG-net effective S$6,000/yr. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers — single application via BGP, no per-seat overage to 150 stakeholders.
gtm.content: Three assets in first 90 days: (1) ungated 'Excel-to-Cap-Table teardown' blog post comparing the typical SG founder's S$8-15k lawyer bill against the PSG-funded bundle; (2) ISCA + SBF co-branded 'PSG-funded ESOP onboarding' webinar with a Big-4 IRAS QEEBR partner as guest; (3) PSG application step-by-step PDF for SG SME founders co-published with a SG-licensed corporate lawyer. Plus: a phantom-vs-QEEBR calculator on the marketing site (per Agent 2 trend on phantom-equity tax tightening); an IRAS Section 14CA worksheet sample download.

attack_plans.icp: Group HR Director or Group Finance lead at a Wavemaker / Vertex / Jungle / Openspace / East Ventures / Insignia / Granite Asia portfolio company (50-200 headcount, post-Series-A, S$10-50M revenue) with a board mandate to deliver clean cap table for Series B due-diligence within 6-12 months.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Per Agent 2: ~600 PE-backed Series-A/B SG SMEs sit inside SAM; per Agent 2's PE-wave trend, ~125 will face forced cap-table-cleanup events in 2026-27 (~180 portfolio companies × 70% formalisation likelihood). At Series-B Ready bundle list S$25k/yr per Agent 3, TAM at full PE-backed slice = 600 × S$12k blended = S$7.2M (calibrated to mixed Founder Bundle + Series-B Ready ARPU); pricing the segment at S$25k/yr would lift slice to S$15M but only ~40% of the cohort buys at the full Series-B-Ready tier in Y1.
attack_plans.why_gap: Carta, Pulley, Ledgy, Qapita, Cake all ship US 83(b) / 409A / EU / India tax engines without IRAS QEEBR registration packs, Section 14CA worksheets, or CPF salary-substitution flags. Big-4 (KPMG, Deloitte, PwC, EY) deliver IRAS-correct ESOP design at SGD 60-280k as project, not platform — six months later the GHRD is back to Excel. BoardRoom and Tricor own the corp-sec relationship but ESOP is hourly add-on, billed inside deal-cost scope. The gap is the missing platform layer: regulator-correct, deal-room-defensible, recurring system-of-record.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 4 on IRAS QEEBR compliance per the canvas + our_score 5 on event_sourced_audit_trail (Carta is 4 with reputational liability; Qapita is 3; nobody else is platform-grade). Series-B Ready SKU per Agent 3 ships GP-ready cap-table file + vesting waterfall reconciliation + multi-jurisdiction tax exposure summary + bespoke legal review of up to 5 non-template grants per year. EDG-eligible consulting wrap (Human Capital Development pillar) layered on PSG-eligible SaaS subscription means the S$25k list lands at S$9.5k effective — one-tenth of the typical pre-Series-B cleanup engagement bill the GP has already priced into deal cost.

gtm.channel: Direct PE-firm partnerships (Wavemaker, Vertex, Jungle, Openspace, East Ventures, Insignia, Granite Asia, Gobi) — preferred-vendor agreement with a portfolio-rate-card S$8k/yr/co bulk pricing. DealStreetAsia + e27 + Tech in Asia content sponsorships timed to Series-B announcement cycles. Co-marketing with KPMG / PwC SG Private Company practice via a referral protocol where post-engagement administration tail flows to Elitez at 1/10th cost.
gtm.pitch: Deliver a Series-B-defensible cap table in 30 days, not 90 — with IRAS QEEBR + Section 14CA + multi-jurisdiction tax exposure baked into the platform, so your GP's audit firm signs off without the S$80-150k cleanup engagement.
gtm.pricing: Series-B Ready list S$25,000/yr; PSG + EDG-net effective S$9,500/yr. Portfolio-rate-card S$8k/yr/co for PE firms with 5+ SG portfolio cos. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers.
gtm.content: Five assets: (1) DealStreetAsia gated whitepaper 'The S$80-150k Series-B Cleanup Tax — and how to avoid it'; (2) Wavemaker / Vertex co-branded portfolio operator briefing (in-person, quarterly); (3) IRAS QEEBR + Section 14CA technical reference for GP audit teams; (4) cap-table-cleanliness self-assessment checklist for portfolio CFOs; (5) sample 30-day Series-B-readiness onboarding plan PDF.

attack_plans.niche_name: Family-business ESOP w/ Big-4 valuation pack
attack_plans.icp: CFO or Group Finance Director of a SG family-owned SME (80-200 headcount, S$20-100M revenue, second-generation now active) introducing ESOP to retain 5+ year non-family senior staff during a generational handover; SBF/SCCCI member firm in F&B groups, logistics, manufacturing, or healthcare services.
attack_plans.tam_reasoning: Per Agent 2 cultural signal (Stephen Riady Centre / NUS Family Firm Survey 2024): ~25% of SG SME SAM is family-owned = ~800 firms in the family-business slice of SAM. At Family Office bundle list S$60k/yr per Agent 3, the addressable revenue at full-slice penetration = 800 × S$60k = S$48M; calibrated to realistic Y1-Y3 capture (20% who are second-gen-active AND past the 'patriarch agreed' threshold) = ~160 firms × S$60k = S$9.6M slice TAM. The cohort that has stalled per Agent 2's 58% second-gen ESOP-rejected stat is recoverable at the right price-and-rigour combination.
attack_plans.why_gap: Big-4 (KPMG, Deloitte, PwC, EY) hold the family-business + valuation seat but engage as project not platform — patriarch + successor + family-lawyer get a binder at SGD 60-280k engagement, then administer in Excel afterward. SaaS platforms (Carta, Qapita, Cake, Pulley, Ledgy) score 0-1 on family_business_governance dimension per the canvas — no trust mechanics, no ratchet/redemption modelling, no two-on-two demo flow for founder + successor. Drew & Napier and Withers KhattarWong hold the family-trust legal seat but no platform either. The gap is platform + Big-4-grade valuation + family-trust governance bundled — exactly what Elitez Group can ship via its existing KPMG relationship + Big-4 audit-bench at SGD 30M+ revenue family clients.
attack_plans.why_we_win: our_score 4 on family_business_governance + our_score 4 on big4_grade_annual_valuation (via Elitez Group's existing KPMG engagement model — KPMG delivers the partner-signed valuation; Elitez ships the platform that survives after the binder). Family Office SKU per Agent 3 includes named relationship manager, two-on-two demo flow, succession-planning workflow, and WDG(JR+) workforce-redesign training wrap (70% rebate, S$50k cap) — the only competitor with WDG(JR+) status in the entire 42-competitor set. EDG + WDG(JR+) drop the S$60k list to S$18k effective, which sits below the typical family-lawyer bill on a single trust amendment.

gtm.channel: SBF + SCCCI Family Business Programme co-sponsorship + Stephen Riady Centre / NUS Family Firm Network engagement + KPMG Private Company Advisory referral protocol (post-engagement administration tail) + targeted introductions through Elitez Group's existing 350-staff client and partner network. CNY / Mid-Autumn family-business roundtable hosted with a named Big-4 partner as guest.
gtm.pitch: Bundle a KPMG-signed annual valuation, family-trust-aware governance templates, and a platform that survives the patriarch's binder — with a named relationship manager who has shipped at least three SG family-business ESOPs.
gtm.pricing: Family Office list S$60,000/yr; EDG + WDG(JR+)-net effective S$18,000/yr. Per Agent 3 recommended_tiers. Component pricing available as upsell variant.
gtm.content: Five assets: (1) SBF / SCCCI co-branded 'Family Business ESOP Playbook' (gated PDF) covering ratchet + redemption + family-share-class mechanics; (2) Stephen Riady Centre / NUS academic paper co-authorship on second-gen ESOP economics; (3) two-on-two demo recording: founder + designated successor walking through 'with vs without ESOP' family economics; (4) named Big-4 partner case study (Elitez Mar 2024 KPMG engagement re-anonymised); (5) family-lawyer review checklist for ESOP scheme deeds.
